tier1_global.name: Global Tier 1 Bank
tier1_global.rationale: Tier 1 banks require strictest tolerances due to systemic risk, high transaction volumes ($50B+ daily), and enhanced regulatory scrutiny (Basel III, FFIEC Large Bank standards). 99% parity threshold reflects zero-tolerance approach for systemically important financial institutions.

tier2_regional.name: Regional Tier 2 Bank
tier2_regional.rationale: Tier 2 banks balance risk management with operational flexibility. Standard Wilson 95% CI threshold appropriate for $1B-$10B asset range. FFIEC IT Handbook allows risk-based approach commensurate with institution size and complexity.

community_bank.name: Community Bank
community_bank.rationale: Community banks (<$1B assets) have lower transaction volumes and less complex operations. FFIEC allows risk-based approach commensurate with size and complexity. 90% threshold reflects proportionate risk management for smaller institutions with limited IT resources.

demo_default.name: Demo Bank (Default Profile)
demo_default.rationale: Default profile for demonstrations and backward compatibility. Uses standard tier 2 thresholds matching original Bob the Judge hardcoded values.
